description: A square ICE-designed 'rogues gallery' card built over a desaturated black-and-white close-up photograph of a young man's face, apparently shot inside a vehicle (a seat headrest is visible behind him). He has short hair, a small beard, an earring, and a visible teardrop-style face tattoo under one eye. The left side carries a dark translucent panel holding cyan/light-blue serif headline text 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL' with a white subhead '1/27/2025 | Chicago, IL'. Below in large white sans-serif: 'Honduran National'. A bulleted 'CRIMES:' list follows: 'Robbery & escape', 'Retail theft', 'Confirmed Sureno gang member'. The official DHS/ICE seal and 'ICE' wordmark sit in the lower-left corner. A bold red diagonal banner across the lower-right corner reads 'CHICAGO'. Visual style is high-contrast monochrome photo with a crime-poster/mugshot aesthetic. The propaganda technique pairs a menacing cropped portrait with a nationality label and a crime list to associate immigrants with gang violence and criminality, branded to a specific enforcement city. Target audience: the public and supporters of stepped-up interior immigration enforcement; intended to publicize and dramatize ICE arrests.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/27/2025 | Chicago, IL; Honduran National; CRIMES: Robbery & escape, Retail theft, Confirmed Sureno gang member; CHICAGO
